No Postman? No Problem — Testing APIs with curl
Sometimes you can't use Postman to test an API and you can't install other tools — maybe you're SSH'd into a server (servers usually have no GUI). That's when you reach for curl.

Postman is one of those tools that pays for itself within the first hour. Before integrating an API into your actual project, just test it in Postman — saves an enormous amount of debug time later. This post is a quick start — installing Postman, creating requests, organizing into collections, setting up environment variables, generating snippet code for your codebase, exporting/importing collections, and running automated tests across a collection.
HMAC Authentication in Postman With Pre-Request Scripts (TDX API Example)

HMAC authentication is common for public APIs that need request-level signing, but Postman doesn't ship with HMAC support out of the box. This post walks through implementing HMAC in Postman via a pre-request script — using Taiwan's TDX (transport data) API as the concrete example, with jsSHA loaded via CDN inside the script.
